description: A combined lecture and seminar course covering modern marine geological processes in a wide range of oceanic environments:, including mid-ocean ridges, mid-plate volcanoes and hot spots, coastlines, continental margins and abyssal plains. Modern methods of data collection and analysis, including the Ocean Drilling Program.

groupFilter1.name: School of Earth and Ocean Sciences

preAndCorequisites:
• Complete all of:
    • EOS201 - Sedimentary Geology (1.5)
    • EOS316 - Igneous and Metamorphic Geology (1.5)
  • or permission of the school.
supplementalNotes:
• Normally offered in alternate years.
  • Students are advised to consult with the department to determine when this is offered.
title: Marine Geology
